title: Comprehensive audit and rewrite of auditing-upstream-drift skill
description: The audit skill needs to be mission-critical complete. Must cover: test execution (brainstorm server, fast skill tests, integration tests), documentation staleness checks, LICENSE/attribution verification, plugin manifest validation, hook functionality testing, skill namespace verification, TodoWrite residue scanning, doc path consistency, and the full test refactoring workflow we just went through. This skill is the quality gate for the entire plugin.
close_reason: Complete rewrite of auditing-upstream-drift skill. Expanded from 4 phases to 8: (1) Plugin infrastructure health — manifest validation, version consistency, hook executable+JSON, settings.json correctness, duplicate hook detection, skills count, LICENSE attribution. (2) Test execution — brainstorm server 25/25, WS protocol 31/31, fast skill tests 9 subtests, optional integration test. (3) Content integrity — TodoWrite residue, stale paths, stale namespaces, beads density 30+, subagent isolation. (4) Progressive skill chain — all pipeline links verified. (5) Upstream superpowers drift — clone+compare. (6) Upstream beads drift — version+features. (7) Documentation accuracy — README, CHANGELOG, CLAUDE.md, SETUP-GUIDE, copied docs. (8) Audit report generation with beads. Plus Quick Audit one-liner for fast checks. Self-verified: Quick Audit passes all checks.

close_reason: Replaced bd prime hooks in .claude/settings.json with beads-superpowers plugin hook (bash ./hooks/session-start). This hook injects the using-superpowers skill + runs bd prime in a single call. Tested: hook outputs valid JSON with skill content and beads context.

title: Fix stale GitHub issue sync and research bd github recipes
description: GitHub sync reopened previously-closed beads as open issues. Need to fix the sync state, then research if beads has a built-in recipe for bd github sync that we could use as a default setup.
close_reason: Fixed GitHub issue sync: 13 stale-open issues closed via gh CLI (bd github sync doesn't propagate local closes to GitHub — it pushes new/updated but doesn't close). Research complete: no built-in GitHub recipe exists in beads. The recipe system supports 4 types (file, hooks, section, multifile) and custom recipes via bd setup --add. GitHub sync is a separate integration, not a recipe — it's configured via bd config set github.* and synced via bd github sync. Custom recipes are stored in .beads/recipes.toml.

title: Migrate beads from embedded Dolt to server mode
description:
Currently using embedded Dolt mode (.beads/metadata.json dolt_mode: embedded). In this mode, bd dolt show/status/push/pull all fail with 'not supported in embedded mode'. No remotes configured.

## Knowledge Base Coverage (from OpenViking)
Extensive existing docs cover this exact problem:
- beads-dolt-external-database-and-github-remote-setup — External DB and GitHub remote approach
- beads-dolt-database-strategy — Centralised Dolt remote architecture (Avengers-au/beads-dolt pattern)
- beads-dolt-notion-troubleshooting-guide — Quick reference recovery checklist
- beads-sync-architecture — Dolt remote setup options (DoltHub, GitHub, filesystem)
- beads-dolt-github-remote-and-notion-sync-setup — What didn't work + setup instructions

## Key Findings from KB
1. The issue is NOT embedded vs server mode per se — it's that embedded mode doesn't support dolt subcommands
2. Need to either: (a) reinitialise with bd init in server mode, or (b) manually update metadata.json dolt_mode and set up dolt sql-server
3. Existing pattern uses GitHub as Dolt remote (Avengers-au/beads-dolt repo with per-project refs)
4. Push performance to GitHub is poor (45-80s) — DoltHub is faster but requires account
5. bd dolt remote add origin <url> configures the remote after server mode is working
6. Always bd dolt commit before bd dolt pull to avoid uncommitted changes errors

## Steps
1. Research how to convert existing embedded DB to server mode (bd migrate? manual metadata edit? bd init --force?)
2. Set up dolt sql-server for this project  
3. Configure remote (GitHub: DollarDill/beads-dolt or Avengers-au/beads-dolt)
4. Verify bd dolt push/pull work
5. Update beads-superpowers SETUP-GUIDE.md if needed

value: beads-superpowers project uses embedded Dolt mode (.beads/metadata.json dolt_mode: embedded). In this mode, bd dolt show/status/push/pull all fail. No remotes configured. Need to migrate to server mode: bd dolt start/stop commands exist but are blocked in embedded mode. The bd migrate command handles DB metadata but not embedded→server conversion. Research needed on bd init --mode=server or manual metadata.json edit + dolt sql-server setup.
